jokes.text: Please, man, I'm from Philly. To me, a wilderness experience is dating a girl who don't shave her legs.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if Will is ready for camping expedition
jokes.punchline: Will equates wilderness to dating someone with unshaved legs
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific joke that establishes Will's street-smart perspective vs. privileged family's outdoor adventure. Excellent economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: My uncle Reggie always insisted the woods were safer than the cities. Until the day he died. - Word? Yo, how'd he die? He was eaten by wolves.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions uncle who thought woods were safer
jokes.punchline: The 'safer' woods killed him via wolves
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic reversal with dark comedy payoff. Well-structured with Will's incredulous response setting up the punchline.

jokes.text: Carlton's log. Earth date: 1992. It's 6:00 a.m. Up with the dawn, the small band of brave souls prepare to pile into their Mercedes.
jokes.setup: Carlton doing dramatic Captain's log style narration
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposition of adventure language with luxury Mercedes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his pompous, dramatic nature applied to mundane camping prep. Mercedes detail undercuts adventure pretensions.

jokes.text: Carlton, you look like Webster on a safari.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably dressed up for camping
jokes.punchline: Comparison to child actor Webster in safari gear
jokes.explanation: Solid visual joke playing on Carlton's small stature and over-preparation. Webster reference dates it but was topical for 1992.

jokes.text: Beauty makeover? With this face? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks about beauty makeover weekend
jokes.punchline: Hilary's supreme confidence that she needs no makeover
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary vanity moment. Predictable but perfectly in character - her narcissism is the entire joke.

jokes.text: Hey, man, I ain't trying to be nowhere around a wind that can knock Uncle Phil to the ground.
jokes.setup: Phil describes powerful winds from camping memories
jokes.punchline: Will points out Phil's considerable size makes this impressive
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Phil's weight without being mean-spirited. Will's street perspective on Phil's camping stories.

jokes.text: And you know how often I'm right. Bye.
jokes.setup: Hilary predicts 90% chance of rain for their trip
jokes.punchline: Her supreme confidence in weather predictions she's presumably always wrong about
jokes.explanation: Ironic confidence from notoriously unreliable Hilary. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: Let's unpack the rain gear. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Hilary's confident wrong predictions
jokes.punchline: Immediate capitulation to her weather forecast despite knowing she's always wrong
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat showing the family's conditioned response to Hilary's pronouncements, even when they know better.

jokes.text: Do you think anyone would drive by the same tree seven times on purpose?
jokes.setup: Phil claims he knows where he's going
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals they've been driving in circles
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that punctures Phil's confidence with specific, absurd detail. Carlton's earnest delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Carlton's log. We're somewhere in the galaxy. My father has no idea where.
jokes.setup: Carlton's dramatic log narration from earlier
jokes.punchline: Now admits they're completely lost in cosmic terms
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the pompous narration to absurd cosmic scale while admitting defeat. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, can you take a stroll into the '90s, please?
jokes.setup: Phil suggests hitchhiking like in the '60s
jokes.punchline: Will points out generational gap and current reality
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor with Will as voice of modern reality vs. Phil's outdated optimism.

jokes.text: We are three black men on the side of a mountain road. The only people that's going to stop... is going to have on sheets and saying stuff like, 'Get them, Jim Bob.'
jokes.setup: Phil wants to hitchhike
jokes.punchline: Will points out racial reality of rural hitchhiking
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversive humor that addresses real racial tensions with sharp wit. Will's street awareness contrasts Phil's naive optimism. Brave and funny social commentary.

jokes.text: Wait, this ain't the Volvo.
jokes.setup: They realize their camping equipment is missing
jokes.punchline: Will realizes they're in the wrong luxury car
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - not only are they lost, they're in the wrong expensive car. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, great idea, Carlton. You can tell them exactly where we are, too. Somewhere in the dark between a rock and a tree.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests calling road service
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically describes their impossibly vague location
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about the uselessness of calling for help when completely lost. Will's practical perspective.

jokes.text: Well, I guess sending for pizza's out.
jokes.setup: Phone service unavailable in wilderness
jokes.punchline: Will's casual dismissal of pizza delivery in the woods
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan humor showing Will's urban mindset applied to wilderness situation. Good economy.

jokes.text: Does Bigfoot have to come down here with a chain saw... before you admit we're in trouble?
jokes.setup: Phil still trying to stay optimistic about their situation
jokes.punchline: Will suggests absurdly extreme scenario needed for Phil to admit problems
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist escalation combining horror movie elements. The specific 'chainsaw' detail makes it more vivid and funny.

jokes.text: You know, I feel really comfortable like this, too, Geoffrey. I mean, I don't need to hide behind cosmetics. I'm beautiful just the way I am.
jokes.setup: Hilary seen without makeup for once
jokes.punchline: Temporary moment of genuine self-acceptance
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bigger payoff when Trevor arrives. Ironic given Hilary's usual vanity - temporary character growth.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. It's Trevor. Quick, get my hot comb.
jokes.setup: Hilary just said she's beautiful without cosmetics
jokes.punchline: Immediately panics and wants to fix her appearance for Trevor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency joke - Hilary's self-acceptance lasts exactly until a man appears. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: They look just like the flowers down at the newsroom. Just be sure to have them back before 10:00.
jokes.setup: Trevor brings flowers for romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Reveals he stole them from work and needs them back
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Trevor's cheap romantic gesture. The casual admission makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I have no life.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks why Geoffrey is working on his day off
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's matter-of-fact admission of emptiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery. Simple but effective - his dry honesty about his sad existence gets the laugh.

jokes.text: On my day off? You'd really let me?
jokes.setup: Hilary asks Geoffrey to answer door
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic excitement about working on day off
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic Geoffrey moment playing on the servant/employer dynamic. His dry delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Or you would be if you combed your hair once in a while. And some lipstick wouldn't kill you, either.
jokes.setup: Jazz calls Hilary beautiful and desirable
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts compliment with appearance criticisms
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz obliviousness - thinks he's being romantic while being insulting. Good backhanded compliment structure.

jokes.text: Carlton's log. I'm wet. I'm wet and I'm cold. I'm wet and I'm cold and I'm in a cave. Shut up, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton's dramatic log narration continuing
jokes.punchline: Escalating misery list interrupted by Will's annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalating structure showing Carlton's deteriorating situation and Will's frustration.

jokes.text: I want to go to college and poke fun at all the kids on financial aid.
jokes.setup: Carlton panicking about dying
jokes.punchline: His life goals include mocking less privileged students
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor revealing Carlton's privileged oblivious worldview even in crisis. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Do you all mind if I get butt naked and roll around in this for a little while?
jokes.setup: Will finds $25,000 in cash
jokes.punchline: His immediate impulse to get naked and roll in money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will Smith character moment - impulsive, physical, and hilariously inappropriate. The specific phrasing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Goodnight, Abe. Goodnight, Alexander. And I can't forget you, Ben.
jokes.setup: Will going to sleep with money
jokes.punchline: Saying goodnight to presidents on bills like bedtime story
jokes.explanation: Clever personification of currency with bedtime ritual structure. Shows Will's relationship with money in character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: A really bad cook?
jokes.setup: Jazz asks if Trevor knows who he is after insult
jokes.punchline: Trevor's innocent guess that cuts deeper
jokes.explanation: Good innocent misunderstanding that accidentally delivers perfect insult. Trevor's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I have never been so insulted in my life. Blind people recognize me.
jokes.setup: Trevor is offended by Jazz's treatment
jokes.punchline: His absurd claim about fame extending to blind people
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist claim about his fame. The specific impossibility makes it funnier and reveals character's delusion.

jokes.text: I put the 'afro' in aphrodisiac.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to convince Hilary he's better lover
jokes.punchline: Forced wordplay connecting his hairstyle to sexual appeal
jokes.explanation: Cheesy wordplay that perfectly fits Jazz's overconfident pickup style. The forced pun is the joke - it's intentionally bad.

jokes.text: Carlton's log. I hate Dad.
jokes.setup: Continuing Carlton's dramatic log entries
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest admission replacing flowery language
jokes.explanation: Perfect evolution of the running gag - Carlton's pretentious narration reduced to blunt honesty. Excellent economy and character development.

jokes.text: Come on, Uncle Phil, at least knock him out first.
jokes.setup: Phil suggests burning Carlton (misunderstanding about money)
jokes.punchline: Will's merciful suggestion to make Carlton unconscious first
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy misunderstanding with Will showing 'mercy' for what he thinks is murder. Good character moment showing Will's warped loyalty.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I can't get a date, either.
jokes.setup: Phil says there are more important things than money
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out he fails at everything valuable
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor from Carlton acknowledging his romantic failures. Undercuts Phil's wisdom with harsh reality.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Ben. You was one of the few white people I could really count on.
jokes.setup: Ben Franklin burning in fire
jokes.punchline: Will's racial commentary about trusting money over people
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with sharp social commentary. Will's honest assessment of race relations through money metaphor is brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: Will's log is about to connect with Carlton's head!
jokes.setup: Carlton's pompous log entry continues
jokes.punchline: Will threatens violence using log pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using Carlton's log format against him. Good physical comedy setup with smart verbal construction.

jokes.text: Perhaps a matinee and a box of Raisinets.
jokes.setup: Phil wanted quality time before college
jokes.punchline: Will suggests much simpler, safer alternative
jokes.explanation: Good practical suggestion showing Will's preference for simple pleasures over adventure. The specific 'Raisinets' detail adds character.

jokes.text: Can we get some Goobers?
jokes.setup: Will mentioning Raisinets for movie bonding
jokes.punchline: Carlton's candy preference even in this serious moment
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Carlton's different taste even in candy. Fits his contrarian nature.

jokes.text: So I used to put on one skate and push with the other foot. Never seemed like I could be fast enough to keep up with the other kids, though.
jokes.setup: Will talking about learning things alone
jokes.punchline: Touching image of makeshift skating method
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment combining humor with pathos. The specific visual is both funny and heartbreaking. Sets up meaningful metaphor.

jokes.text: Thanks for my other skate, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Will's metaphor about one skate growing up
jokes.punchline: Phil gave him the completion/family he needed
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff to the skating metaphor. Transforms comedy setup into profound statement about family and belonging. Exceptional writing.

jokes.text: Silly rabbit, Trix are for kids.
jokes.setup: Carlton talking about witnessing injustice and nightmares
jokes.punchline: The 'injustice' was Trix rabbit being denied cereal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - builds up serious trauma only to reveal it's about cereal commercial. Perfect Carlton innocence and absurdist escalation. Classic moment.

jokes.text: How come they just couldn't give him some cereal?
jokes.setup: Carlton's trauma about Trix rabbit
jokes.punchline: Genuine empathy for cartoon character's plight
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing Carlton's genuine concern for cartoon injustice. His innocent worldview makes absurdity touching and funny.

jokes.text: Vivian, I'm not a machine!
jokes.setup: Phil waking up from camping nightmare
jokes.punchline: Sleep-talking marital complaint about sexual expectations
jokes.explanation: Good adult humor about marital dynamics delivered as sleep talking. Reveals Phil's domestic anxieties beyond camping disaster.

jokes.text: If I ever agree to go camping with you all... just hold my head underwater till I stop struggling.
jokes.setup: Relief at surviving camping disaster
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic vow never to camp again
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Will's characteristic dramatic flair. The specific drowning imagery makes it more vivid and funny.

jokes.text: He's trying to find his way back to the circus.
jokes.setup: Introducing Carlton to news reporter
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Carlton belongs in circus
jokes.explanation: Standard short joke about Carlton's appearance/behavior. Works as throwaway line but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Terrible. Uncle Backdraft made us burn it.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks how it feels to be rich
jokes.punchline: Will's nickname for Phil and complaint about burning money
jokes.explanation: Good nickname for Phil connecting to fire theme. Will's bitter tone about the loss adds character depth.

jokes.text: I must have miscounted. Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Learning they burned $50,000 not $25,000
jokes.punchline: Will's casual dismissal of massive calculation error
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to devastating news. Will's casual 'oh well' attitude makes the massive loss funnier.

jokes.text: How to pinch a tent? Come here, you cute little tent. I'm gonna pinch you. You is a cute little tent.
jokes.setup: Phil's camping wisdom about 'pinching' tents
jokes.punchline: Will misinterprets as literally pinching tent like baby talk
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay confusion with Will's childlike interpretation. Works as tag but not particularly strong.
